translations.translation:
Sun Huan (Shuwu)
孫桓 (叔武)
Lived AD 197 or 198–after 222
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 51 (Wu 6)
byZL181
onMay 12th, 2023
AbstractEntity::setUpdateForRefresh: 'update_for_refresh' value 'NULL' changed to 'true' via 'loadFlags::$this->update_for_refresh'
AbstractEntity::updateTimestamp: Timestamp 'published_at' changed to '1683899881' from 'NULL' via 'AbstractEntity::loadEntityCache::data[db][*]'
AbstractEntity::updateTimestamp: Timestamp 'rendered_at' changed to '1757966991' from 'NULL' via 'AbstractEntity::loadEntityCache::data[db][*]'
AbstractEntity::updateTimestamp: Timestamp 'updated_at' changed to '1740669211' from 'NULL' via 'AbstractEntity::loadEntityCache::data[db][*]'
AbstractEntity::updateTimestamp: Timestamp 'created_at' changed to '1740615968' from 'NULL' via 'AbstractEntity::loadEntityCache::data[db][*]'
Sun Huan, styledShuwu, was the son of[Sun] He.A
TheBook of Wustates:[Sun] Hehad four sons. The eldest was[Sun] Zhu,ChiefofQu’e[曲阿長]. The next was[Sun] Yi,ChiefofHaiyan[海鹽長]. They both died young. The next was[Sun] Huan. His appearance was upstanding and upright, and his mind was clever and clear. He had extensive knowledge, had a strong memory, and was capable of giving answers in debates.[Sun] Quanoften praised him to be theYan Yuanof his relatives,1and nominated him to beCommandant of the Martial Guards. He followed in the suppression ofGuan YuatHuarongand enticed the remaining fellows of[Guan] Yu[to surrender], gaining five thousand people as well as numerous oxen, horses, andweapons.•••
ForSun Quanto have seenSun Huanas theYan Huiof the Sun clan is noteworthy because the father ofSun Huan,Sun He, was not born into the Sun clan, but rather adopted into it, although this would not be controversial back then: adoptions were not an uncommon custom.↩
When he was twenty-five, he was conferred asHousehold General who Secures the East, and he andLu XunopposedLiu Beitogether.2The army of[Liu] Beiwas massive, and they filled the hills and valleys.[Sun] Huandevoted his strength and exerted himself, and he and[Lu] Xunjoined forces.[Liu] Beiwas thus defeated and he retreated.[Sun] Huancut open and went through the roads of Kui [夔],3thus obstructing the narrow key points. However,[Liu] Beiscaled hills and dangerous terrain, thus barely escaping, then angrily sighed, “When I was in the capital during the past,[Sun] Huanwas just a little boy. Now he has forced me into thissituation!”•••
The appointment was probably made during the campaign ofLiu BeiagainstSun Quanthat lasted from the years 221 to 222. SinceSun Huanwas twenty-four at the time (with one year subtracted to account for East Asian age reckoning), he would have been born in either the year 197 or 198.↩
Sun Huanand his army traversed the roads of the ancient state of Kui [夔國], which was based around the area, by cutting through something to clear land. They presumably cut through the hills.↩
[Sun] Huan, for his merits, was conferred asGeneral who Establishes Might, enfeoffed asMarquisofDantu[丹徒侯], then went downward to superviseNiuzhu[牛渚]. He was constructing a fort along theHeng river[橫江] when he happened todie.B
TheBook of Wustates: The younger brother of[Sun] Huanwas[Sun] Jun, styledShuying. He was magnanimous and very tolerant by personality. He was talented and well-versed in civil and military matters. He served asHousehold General who Settles Might, and he garrisoned and defended high fences.4He died in the thirteenth year of theera [250]. His older son,[Sun] Jian[孫建], inherited the peerage and served asGeneral who Pacifies Caitiffs. His younger son,[Sun] Zhen, served asGeneral who Guards the South. The son of[Sun] Zhenwas[Sun] Cheng, styledXianshi.•••
The implication here is that he was a border general, as he was defending places that required fences to block off intruders.↩
TheBiographies of Scholarsstate:[Sun] Chengwas fond of learning and had literary talent. He wrote thePoem of the Florescent Light, which has passed onto this era.5He served asAttending Gentleman at the Yellow Gates. He andGu Rongboth were attending ministers. During the era of theMarquis who Returns to the Mandate, many of the interior attendants committed grave crimes. Only[Gu] Rongand[Sun] Chengremained intact. The duo were often ordered to record matters, with[Sun] Chenggiving the replies to the questions given byGu [Rong]. An edictstated:•••
The poem of the same title created in the Western Jin dynasty is extant today, but it is attributed toPan Yue.↩
“From now and thereafter, those appointed toAttending Gentlemanshould all be those akin to the incumbents, imperial relative[Sun] ChengandGu Rong.”6•••
The Chinese character that I translated into “serving” is Jin [今], which actually means “currently”. However, I instead used “serving” because I felt like using a present participle was the least awkward way to render the sentence, which is stating thatSun ChengandGu Rongserved “currently” as attendants.↩
WhenWuwas pacified, he went toLuo[洛], then he served asPrefectofZhuo[涿令] inFanyang[范陽] and made many praiseworthy accomplishments there. During theera,Lu Jiserved asGrand Marshalto thePrinceofChengdu[成都王], and invited[Sun] Chengto serve as aMajor.[Sun] Chengand[Lu] Jiwould be both killed.
